table-reference.title: List of amendments - NCC 2025 Volume One

row.entry: A new sub-clause has been added to specify that Performance Solutions for the structural reliability of components must be at least equivalent to solutions developed using the Deemed-to-Satisfy Provisions. A Note has been included to outline transitional arrangements.

A limitations statement has been added that prohibits the use of A2G2(2)(c)—Expert Judgement—in Performance Solutions for certain Performance Requirements, to the extent these Performance Requirements relate to structural or fire safety.

Multiple amendments, including new Notes and Exemptions, to permit the use of an edition of a document edition found on the register of alternative referenced documents as an alternative to the edition listed in Schedule 2.

Multiple amendments to allow a building material, component or assembly to be deemed to have a fire hazard property where it differs in a minor degree to a tested prototype, as confirmed by an Accredited Testing Laboratory.
A new sub-clause has been added to specify methods for determining combustibility.

Various amendments are included that improve the robustness of Performance Solutions regarding structural reliability by necessitating use of B1V1 and appropriate allowances to demonstrate achievement of specific reliability levels. A Note has been included to outline transitional arrangements.
This Performance Requirement has been deleted as a consequence of amendments to B1P1. Following Performance Requirements of Part B1 have been renumbered accordingly.
Revised to incorporate component variability factors, specify further design actions and align with amendments to B1P1.
Amended to include accommodation of 10-year deflection for when designing structural substrates so that surface falls required by Part F1 are maintained.
Amended to reflect revised wind regions.
New subclause included referencing AS 1562.1 and Specification 4 for metal roofing for roof construction in cyclonic areas.
Reference to ‘small sized, low speed automatic lifts’ has been removed.
Amended to reflect revised wind regions.
Amended to require that EW classification testing be undertaken by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A Note has been included to outline transitional arrangements.
Amended to clarify reference to pressurising equipment for all parts of an exit.
Amended to clarify extent of external walls to which this provision applies.
Amended to extend concession to wiring, sensors and the like for stair pressurisation systems.
Amended to clarify reference to opening protection methods specified in AS 1668.1.
Amended to clarify when Class 7 parts may be regarded as Class 2 and/or Class 3 parts for the purpose of determining fire-resistance requirements.
New provisions included to allow the use of fire-protected steel in Type A construction in certain circumstances.
Amended such that open-deck carparks that are part of multi-classified buildings no longer receive concession unless adequately sprinkler protected, among other requirements.
Amended to clarify the use and class of the lowest and remaining storeys and that the requirements of (2)(c) only apply to the storey’s loadbearing elements.
Amended such that open-deck carparks that are part of multi-classified buildings no longer receive concession unless adequately sprinkler protected, among other requirements.
Amended to clarify the use and class of the lowest and remaining storeys and that the requirements of (c) only apply to the storey’s loadbearing elements.
Amended to require that testing to determine the fire hazard properties listed be undertaken by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A Note has been included to outline transitional arrangements.
Amended to clarify smoke-proof wall specifications, including specification for smoke dampers.
Amended to clarify the specification for smoke dampers.
The concession from smoke reservoir provision for doors serving zone pressurised fire compartments has been removed.
A limitation has been introduced to clarify the types of ramps for which handrails and other features must be provided.
A new Performance Requirement has been introduced to require the provision of wayfinding signage.
Amended to include fire compartments served by horizontal exit.
Amended to provide concession for threshold ramps.
Reference to Class 9a and Class 9c buildings has been removed.
Concessions have been introduced for step ramps, threshold ramps, kerb ramps and handrails provided under D3D22.
Amended to extend application to Class 2, 3, 6 and 7a buildings and enhance re-entry provision.
A new requirement has been introduced for the identification of stairways, floor levels and sole-occupancy units through wayfinding signage.
Amended to include reference to AS 2118.6.
Amended to require that above ground pipes and fittings meet certain material requirements.
New subclause to exempt fire-separated areas less than 100 m2 where certain conditions are met.
Due amendment to E1D9, reference to open-deck carparks that are within multi-classified buildings has been removed.
Amended to incorporate concession for certain Class 8 electricity network substations.
Amended to remove concession for open-deck carparks that are within multi-classified buildings and require sprinklers in fire compartments where carstackers are installed.
Clarification amendments, including specification for smoke dampers.
‘Miscellaneous exhaust air systems’ changed to ‘ventilation systems’ to align with terminology in AS 1668.1.
Amended to clarify that requirements apply to carpark ventilation systems.
‘Miscellaneous exhaust air systems’ changed to ‘ventilation systems’ to align with terminology in AS 1668.1.
Amended to clarify extent of application.
Amended to clarify application of the floor area threshold to the Class 9b part.
Amended to require contrast between lettering and background.
Amended to clarify that the requirement to distribute emergency lifts among available shafts does not increase the number of required emergency lifts.
Small sized, low speed automatic lifts are no longer permitted.
Amended to prevent multiple fire service recall switches.
New requirements added for on-site storage of fire service lift keys.
New requirement added for on-site storage of fire service lift keys.
Amended to include reference to SA TS 5367 for hybrid photoluminescent exit signs. Specification 25 remains applicable for passive photoluminescent exit signs.
Reference to open-deck carparks is removed on account of amendment to E1D9.
Amended to clarify application of nominated air change rate.
Amended to clarify application of AS 1670.1.
‘Miscellaneous exhaust air systems’ changed to ‘ventilation systems’ and other amendments to align with and clarify application of AS 1668.1.
Dimensions amended to align with other standards and clarification amendments.
Amended to align with changes to E4D8.
Part F1 has been re-named as a consequence of Section F restructuring.
Amended to incorporate the new defined term ‘water’.
Revised to reflect the outcomes of project work.
Revised to reflect the outcomes of project work.
Re-written following project work that improved water management provisions. F1P1 now incorporates requirements previously found in F1P2 and F1P3.
F1P4 of NCC 2022 has been rewritten and relocated to F1P2.
Deleted on account of amendments to F1P1.
F3V1 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1V1.
Amended following project work that improved water management provisions.
A new clause has been inserted to require falls and drainage for concrete roofs, balconies, podiums and the like.
A new clause has been inserted to specify construction of concrete roofs, balconies, podiums and the like.
F1D4 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D6 and amended to include further location requirements.
F1D5 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D7 and amended to include membrane installation requirements.
F1D6 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D8 and amended to remove concession for certain Class 7 and 8 buildings.
A new clause has been included to specify surface finish requirements.
F3D2 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D12.
F3D3 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D13.
F3D4 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D14 and amended to remove concession for certain Class 7 and 8 buildings.
F3D5 of NCC 2022 has been relocated to F1D8 and amended to remove concession for certain Class 7 and 8 buildings and replace ‘garage’ with the defined term ‘private garage’.
Part F3 has been deleted as a consequence of incorporation of requirements in Part F1.
Amended to extend the requirement for a means of disposal of sanitary products to all sanitary facilities. Also, amended to require a dispenser for sanitary products in facilities for use by females.
New provisions included to allow, under a Deemed-to-Satisfy Solution, the provision of all gender sanitary facilities in lieu of sanitary facilities for males and females.
Amended to require additional closet pans for female patrons.
Amended as a consequence of new provisions that permit all gender sanitary facilities in lieu of sanitary facilities for males and females.
Amended as a consequence of new provisions that permit all gender sanitary facilities in lieu of sanitary facilities for males and females.
Amended to specify the construction of all gender facilities.
Amended to clarify application and to include reference to AS 1668.4 as an optional specification for natural ventilation provision.
The word ‘commercial’ has been deleted so that all kitchens meeting the equipment power threshold require exhaust hood systems.
Amended to provide further membrane vapour permeance specifications, dependent on wall construction, and further details for cavity construction.
In NCC 2022, F8D5 applied to all roof spaces. In NCC 2025, F8D5 is amended and applies to roof spaces where the primary insulation layer is not parallel to the roof plane.
Added to specify requirements for roof spaces where the primary insulation layer is parallel to the roof plane.
Amended such that clause 2.3.1 of AS 1926.1 applies to boundary barriers.
New provision included to provide a Deemed-to-Satisfy Solution for discharge of swimming pool drainage.
A new Note is introduced referring to Section J of NCC 2022 Amendment 2 for Class 2 buildings. Accordingly, references to Class 2 buildings throughout Section J of NCC 2025 are removed.
Amended, following project work, to reflect updated performance targets.
Amended to allow for scenarios where the full extent of on-site renewable energy generation or storage is already realised.
Amended to reflect updated performance targets and include further specifications for the energy model.
Amended to remove the ‘Design & As Built’ option.
Amended to include further specifications for the proposed and reference buildings.
Amended on account of changes to J1P1.
Amended on account of changes in Part J4 and new requirements regarding onsite renewable energy systems.
Amended to specify application to metal-framed flat, skillion and cathedral roofs.
J3D6 of NCC 2022 has been deleted on account of thermal bridging control being inherent in compliance with wall-glazing U-value requirements.
Amended to clarify solar admittance calculation method.
Amended to clarify the application of slab-edge insulation.
Amended to include more detailed specifications for net equivalent energy usage calculations.
Amended to provide further specification for the installation of insulation.
Amended to specify various Total R-Values for various scenarios.
Total system U-Values, solar admittance values and R-values for walls and glazing have been updated.
Amended to incorporate reference to the external building fabric of Class 4 parts.
Concession for non-conditioned spaces and climate zones 1-3 has been removed.
Concession for climate zones 1-3 has been removed.
Extended to apply to external fabric of Class 4 parts of buildings, and include concessions for certain temporary unconditioned spaces.
Concession for conditioned spaces and climate zones 1-3 removed. Also, concession introduced for continuously operating fans.
Various amendments, including limiting of heating and cooling at zone level, to improve efficiency through air-conditioning system control.
Various amendments, including introduction of further scenarios where modulating control is required, to improve efficiency through mechanical ventilation system control.
Various amendments, including removal of concessions for low static pressure systems and low supply air volume systems, to improve efficiency of fans and ductwork.
Concession for MEPS certified systems removed.
Additional efficiency requirements added for certain pump systems. Change in terminology from constant and variable speed to constant and variable flow.
Various amendments for improving efficiency of air-conditioning systems. Addition of requirements for electrical system readiness where gas space heating is used.
Updated to include further specification for the efficiency of refrigerant chillers.
Updated to include further specification for the efficiency of unitary air-conditioning systems.
Various amendments, including variable speed operation requirements for three-phase motor fans, for the efficiency of heat rejection equipment.
Lighting timers removed and motion detectors replaced with demand-operated controls.
Various amendments, including further situations for which demand operated control must be provided, for the efficiency of lighting control.
A new clause added to specify this part does not apply a Class 4 part of a building.
New requirements for heated water pipework insulation and electrical system readiness where gas heated water systems are installed.
Various gas heater minimum gross thermal efficiencies are replaced with a single value of 90%. New requirements for electrical system readiness for buildings with gas heated swimming pools.
Various gas heater minimum gross thermal efficiencies are replaced with a single value of 90%. New requirements for electrical system readiness for buildings with gas heated spa pools.
Amended to require and specify an Environmental Management Information System facility for energy monitoring.
Amended to require installation of on-site solar photovoltaic systems, or equivalent renewable energy generation systems.
Amended to include future electrification readiness where gas systems are installed and require installation of on-site renewable generation systems.S34C2
Further specifications for the reference building have been included.
Amended to require the use of climatic data and specify air-conditioning parameters. Greenhouse gas emission factors for electricity in Table S34C3 have been updated.
Amended to include electric vehicle charging.
Various operation and operation profiles have been revised.
Amended to allow for shading of glazing by vertical projections.
Amended to include the scope for manual switches, time switches and demand-operated controls.
Specifications for lighting timers, included in previous editions, have been removed. Manual switch specifications have been introduced for NCC 2025.
Amended to require the ability to program public holidays into time switches. Manual switch specifications have been removed.
Specifications for motion detectors, included in previous editions, have been removed.
Time delay for on/off operation has been removed, as has differential lux specifications for sensor control.
A new specification has been included for specifying the method of calculating fan power ratio for assessing compliance with J6D5.
A new specification has been included for specifying the method of calculating the climate specific part load value for chillers for assessing compliance with J6D11.
A new specification has been included for specifying the method of comparison of zone loads in a proposed building and its corresponding reference building.
Carbon dioxide equivalent per square metre hour (CO2-e/m2.hr) has been added.
Watt peak per meter squared output of a solar photovoltaic panel (Wp/m2) has been added.
A new defined term, ‘allotment’, has been added.
A defined term, ‘ancillary components’, has been added.
The defined term ‘cavity’ has been amended to cover additional wall types.
A new defined term, ‘climate specific part load value’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘collected’, has been added.

The defined term ‘combustible’ has been amended to require that determination using AS 1530.1 is to be undertaken by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.
The defined term ‘condensation’ has been amended to replace ‘moisture’ with ‘liquid water’.
A defined term, ‘control layer’, has been added.

The defined term ‘critical radiant flux’ has been amended to require that determination using AS ISO 9239.1 is to be undertaken by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.
Amended to reflect updated wind classes B1 and B2.
A new defined term, ‘drainage system’, has been added.
A defined term, ‘drained’, has been added.
The defined term ‘envelope’ has been amended.
The defined term ‘fire hazard properties’ has been amended to align methods of determination.
A new defined term, ‘fire-protected steel’, has been added.

The defined term ‘fire-source feature’ has been amended to include the construction edge or perimeter of another building on the allotment, which is not a Class 10 building, which has a use that constitutes a fire load.

The defined term ‘flammability Index’ has been amended to require that determination using AS 1530.2 is to be made by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.
A new defined term, ‘kerb ramp’, has been added.
The defined term ‘mezzanine’ has been amended for clarity.

The defined term ‘non-combustible’ has been amended to require that determination using AS 1530.1 is to be made by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.
Explanatory information has been provided to assist with interpretation.
The defined term, ‘Point of connection’, has been amended to incorporate rainwater storage.
A new defined term, ‘rainwater’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘redirected’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘rising damp’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘self-draining’, has been added.
The defined term ‘small sized, low-speed automatic lift’ has been deleted.

The defined term ‘Smoke-Developed Index’ has been amended to require that determination using AS/NZS 1530.3 is to be made by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.

The defined term ‘smoke development rate’ has been amended to require that determination using AS ISO 9239.1 is to be made by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.
A new defined term, ‘Solar Reflectance Index’, has been added.

The defined term ‘Spread-of-Flame Index’ has been amended to require that determination using AS 1530.3 is to be made by an Accredited Testing Laboratory. A transition period has been included for this amendment.
A new defined term, ‘step ramp’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘stormwater’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘structural substrate’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘sub-surface water’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘surface finish’, has been added.
The defined term ‘surface water’ has been amended for clarity.
A new defined term, ‘surface water seepage’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘threshold ramp’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘Total Solar Reflectance (TSR)’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘water’, has been added.
A new defined term, ‘water services overflow’, has been added.

The 2024 edition of AS ISO 717 Part 1 ‘Acoustics — Rating of sound insulation in buildings and of building elements — Airborne sound insulation’ has been referenced. The associated note (Note 1) has been updated accordingly.
The 2024 edition of AS ISO 717 Part 2 ‘Acoustics — Rating of sound insulation in buildings and of building elements — Impact sound insulation’ has been referenced.
Amdts 1 and 2 of the 2021 edition of AS/NZS 1170 Part 2 ‘Structural design actions — Wind actions’ have been referenced.
The 2024 edition of AS 1170 Part 4 ‘Structural design actions — Earthquake actions in Australia’ has been referenced.

The 2024 edition of AS/NZS 1530 Part 1 ‘Methods for fire tests on building materials, components and structures — Combustibility test for materials (ISO 1182:2020, NEQ)’ has been referenced. A note concerning existing tests (Note 2) has been incorporated accordingly.

Amdt 1 of the 2018 edition of AS 1530 Part 8.1 ‘Methods for fire tests on building materials, components and structures — Tests on elements of construction for buildings exposed to simulated bushfire attack — Radiant heat and small flaming sources’ has been referenced. A note concerning existing tests (Note 2) has been incorporated accordingly.
The 2024 edition of AS 1668 Part 2 ‘The use of ventilation and air conditioning in buildings — Mechanical ventilation in buildings’ has been referenced.
The 2024 edition of AS 1668 Part 4 ‘The use of ventilation and air conditioning in buildings — Natural ventilation of buildings’ has been referenced.
The 2024 edition of AS 1670 Part 1 ‘Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems — System design, installation and commissioning — Fire’ has been referenced.
The 2024 edition of AS 1670 Part 3 ‘Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems — System design, installation and commissioning — Fire alarm monitoring’ has been referenced.

The 2024 edition of AS 1670 Part 4 ‘Fire detection, warning, control and intercom systems — System design, installation and commissioning — Emergency warning and intercom systems’ has been referenced.
Amdt 1 of the 2021 edition of AS 1684 Part 2 ‘Residential timber-framed construction — Non-cyclonic areas’ has been referenced.
Amdt 1 of the 2021 edition of AS 1684 Part 3 ‘Residential timber-framed construction — Cyclonic areas’ has been referenced.
The 2024 edition of AS 1684 Part 4 ‘Residential timber-framed construction — Simplified — Non-cyclonic areas’ has been referenced.
The 2024 edition of AS 1926 Part 1 ‘Swimming pool safety — Safety barriers for swimming pools’ has been referenced.
The 2026 edition of AS 2118 Part 6 ‘Automatic fire sprinkler systems — Combined sprinkler and hydrant systems in multistorey buildings’ has been referenced.
The 2006 edition of AS 2200 ‘Design charts for water supply and sewerage (incorporating amendment 1)’ has been referenced.
The 2025 edition of AS/NZS 3500 Part 1 ‘Plumbing and drainage — Water services’ has been referenced.
The 2025 edition of AS/NZS 3500 Part 2 ‘Plumbing and drainage — Sanitary plumbing and drainage’ has been referenced.
The 2025 edition of AS/NZS 3500 Part 3 ‘Plumbing and drainage — Stormwater drainage’ has been referenced.
The 2025 edition of AS/NZS 3500 Part 4 ‘Plumbing and drainage — Heated water services’ has been referenced.
The 2023 edition of AS 3786 ‘Smoke alarms using scattered light, transmitted light or ionization’ has been referenced. The note regarding existing tests (Note 7) has been updated accordingly.
Amdt 1 of the 2018 edition of AS/NZS 4020 ‘Testing of products for use in contact with drinking water’ has been referenced.
Amdt 1 of the 2021 edition of AS 4055 ‘Wind loads for housing’ has been referenced.
Amdt 1 of the 2018 edition of AS/NZS 4859 Part 1 ‘Thermal insulation materials for buildings — General criteria and technical provisions’ has been referenced.
The 2023 edition of AS 5346 ‘Exterior insulation and finish cladding systems’ has been referenced.
The 2022 edition of AS/NZS 5601 Part 1 ‘Gas installations — General installations (incorporating amendments 1 and 2)’ has been referenced.
The 2023 edition of AS/NZS ISO 5151 ‘Non-ducted air conditioners and heat pumps — Testing and rating for performance’ has been referenced.
The 2023 edition of AS/NZS ISO 13256.1 ‘Water source heat pumps — Testing and rating for performance — Water-to-air and brine-to-air heat pumps’ has been referenced.
The 2019 edition of ASTM-E1980-11 ‘Standard Practice for Calculating Solar Reflectance Index of Horizontal and Low Sloped Opaque Surfaces’ has been referenced.

The 2022 edition of EN14511-2 ‘Air-conditioners, liquid chilling packages and heat pumps for space heating and cooling and process chillers, with electricity driven compressors — Test conditions’ has been referenced.

The 2022 edition of EN14825 ‘Air conditioners, liquid chilling packages and heat pumps, with electrically driven compressors, for space heating and cooling, commercial and process cooling — Testing and rating at part load conditions and calculation of seasonal performance’ has been referenced.
The 2024 edition of NSF/ANSI/CAN 372 ‘Drinking Water System Components — Lead Content’ has been referenced. A note regarding existing tests (Note 10) has been incorporated.
The 2021 edition of SA TS 5367 ‘Photoluminescent exit signage — Product specification, installation and operation’ has been referenced.
